nodes.title: SendCutSend
nodes.summary: A custom-parts provider with an official web upload and quote flow for documented 2D vector and STEP/STP inputs, especially relevant to sheet cutting and supported forming services.

recommendations.statement: Shortlist SendCutSend for compatible sheet or profile work only after matching the exact service, file, material, thickness, feature, and tolerance guidance live.
applicability: The part fits a currently documented SendCutSend process
A clean accepted 2D profile or supported STEP/STP model can be prepared
counterconditions: The route depends on STL input, an unsupported process, or unconfirmed live material and feature constraints

alternatives.reason: Protolabs is an alternative for a different mix of machining, additive, molding, and sheet services.

relationships.description: SendCutSend is a provider candidate for selected custom-part processes.
Official setup guidance lists DXF among accepted 2D vector inputs.
Official setup guidance lists STEP and STP among accepted 3D inputs.
Protolabs offers a different documented process and file mix.

capabilities.label: Sheet profile and forming quote
capabilities.description: The official upload flow accepts documented 2D vector and STEP/STP files for compatible cutting and supported service configurations.

knownConstraints.statement: Official 3D-file guidance requires STEP or STP for supported 3D services and explicitly does not accept STL for that path.

handoffs.purpose: Upload proprietary part files and configure a live manufacturing quote.

unknowns.statement: Part price, minimums, taxes, and quote validity are live and must not be persisted as provider facts.
Material, thickness, process, finish, and service availability must be checked in the live quote flow.
Shipping destination, eligibility, timing, and terms must be reconfirmed for the captain's actual destination.
Tolerance capability must be checked for the exact process, material, thickness, feature, and service combination.

nodes.title: Protolabs
nodes.summary: A digital manufacturing provider documenting CNC machining, additive manufacturing, sheet fabrication, and molding services with web model upload, quoting, and DFM feedback paths.

recommendations.statement: Shortlist Protolabs when its current service and accepted-file documentation fits, then confirm facility, material, tolerance, inspection, and quote details live.
applicability: The part matches a currently documented Protolabs service
A supported solid or mesh artifact and any required drawing can be prepared
counterconditions: The required process, material, geography, inspection, or account terms are not confirmed in the live flow

alternatives.reason: Xometry is an alternative broad manufacturing quote network with different live capability and supplier conditions.

relationships.description: Protolabs is a provider candidate for multiple custom-part processes.
Protolabs documents CNC machining among its services.
Official file guidance lists STEP among accepted solid model formats.
Xometry is an alternative broad manufacturing provider network.

capabilities.label: CNC machining
capabilities.description: Protolabs documents CNC machining and accepts supported 3D CAD model formats through its digital manufacturing workflow.

capabilities.label: 3D printing
capabilities.description: Protolabs documents industrial additive manufacturing services and accepts STL in addition to supported solid model formats.

knownConstraints.statement: Protolabs states that it does not provide part-design services, so the user must arrive with a manufacturable 3D CAD model.

handoffs.purpose: Upload proprietary models and drawings for DFM feedback and a live quote.

unknowns.statement: Current quote price, minimums, taxes, and validity must be obtained from the live quoting flow.
Process, facility, network, material, finish, and production availability must be confirmed live.
Shipping eligibility, destination, timing, and provider terms must be checked for the actual order.
The exact inspection report, sampling, evidence, and eligibility must be selected and confirmed live.

nodes.title: Xometry
nodes.summary: A manufacturing network documenting CNC, additive, sheet, molding, and other process capabilities through a web quoting flow with process-specific accepted inputs.

recommendations.statement: Shortlist Xometry for a documented process only after checking process-specific file support and live supplier, material, inspection, geography, and quote conditions.
applicability: The required process appears in the current official capability catalog
A process-compatible CAD artifact and drawing can be prepared
counterconditions: The decision depends on an unverified customer manufacturing API or assumed supplier capability

alternatives.reason: Fictiv is an alternative broad manufacturing network with its own process and file constraints.

relationships.description: Xometry is a provider candidate for multiple custom-part processes.
Xometry documents CNC machining among its capability catalog.
Official format guidance includes STEP for supported process paths.
Fictiv is an alternative manufacturing network.

capabilities.label: CNC machining
capabilities.description: Xometry documents CNC machining and a web quote flow accepting process-compatible 3D CAD files including STEP.

capabilities.label: 3D printing
capabilities.description: Xometry's official capability catalog lists multiple plastic, resin, and metal additive manufacturing services.

knownConstraints.statement: Accepted file types and requirements vary by manufacturing process and must be selected from current process-specific guidance.

handoffs.purpose: Upload proprietary CAD and drawings to configure a process-specific live quote.

unknowns.statement: Live price, supplier allocation, minimums, taxes, and quote validity are not stored in Possible.
Supplier, process, material, finish, certification, and capacity availability must be checked live.
Shipping destination, supplier geography, timing, tariffs, and terms must be reconfirmed for the quote.
No customer manufacturing-order API is asserted by this record; any API access requires separate official verification.

nodes.title: Fictiv
nodes.summary: A manufacturing network documenting machining, molding, additive, casting, and sheet services through a web quote path with process-specific file and assembly constraints.

recommendations.statement: Shortlist Fictiv only when the current process and file guidance fits the part, then live-check supplier, material, drawing, inspection, geography, and quote conditions.
applicability: A documented Fictiv process matches the part route
Required models and supporting drawings can satisfy current file guidance
counterconditions: Assembly, file, material, or inspection requirements are outside the confirmed live capability

alternatives.reason: Xometry is an alternative manufacturing network with different process and supplier conditions.

relationships.description: Fictiv is a provider candidate for multiple custom-part processes.
Fictiv documents attaching drawings to communicate manufacturing requirements.
Xometry is an alternative manufacturing network.

capabilities.label: Custom manufacturing quote
capabilities.description: Fictiv documents a web quote route across multiple manufacturing capabilities using supported process-specific CAD inputs.

knownConstraints.statement: Supported file formats and assembly handling vary by process, so the current official file-format guidance must be consulted before upload.

handoffs.purpose: Upload proprietary CAD and drawings to configure a live manufacturing quote.

unknowns.statement: Live quote price, minimums, taxes, and validity are not persisted as provider facts.
Supplier, process, material, finish, inspection, and capacity availability must be checked live.
Supplier and shipping geography, timing, terms, and destination eligibility require live confirmation.
Inspection service, sampling, documentation, and process eligibility must be selected and verified live.

nodes.title: Sculpteo
nodes.summary: An additive manufacturing provider documenting online 3D printing services, multiple model formats, and partner API services whose current access and effects require direct confirmation.

recommendations.statement: Shortlist Sculpteo for additive work only after confirming the exact process, format, material, finish, geography, and quote; treat partner API access as unverified until confirmed.
applicability: The part follows an additive manufacturing route
The current online service accepts the prepared model format
counterconditions: The route assumes API access, material, certification, or destination support that has not been confirmed live

alternatives.reason: Protolabs is an alternative provider with documented additive and other process paths.

relationships.description: Sculpteo is a provider candidate for additive custom-part processes.
Sculpteo documents professional additive manufacturing services including powder-bed options.
Protolabs is an alternative provider with additive services.

capabilities.label: 3D printing
capabilities.description: Sculpteo documents online additive manufacturing from uploaded 3D model files across its current service catalog.

knownConstraints.statement: Sculpteo describes API services as a partner offering that requires contact, so access and supported operations cannot be assumed from public documentation alone.

handoffs.purpose: Upload a proprietary additive model and configure a live manufacturing quote.

unknowns.statement: Live price, minimums, taxes, and quote validity must be obtained from the current quote flow.
Process, material, color, finish, inspection, and production availability must be checked live.
Shipping destination, timing, eligibility, and terms must be confirmed for the actual handoff.
Partner API eligibility, authentication, operations, and external effects require direct current confirmation.
